points: Standardized SI units like the metre (m) replace irregular non-standard units (balisht, angula) to ensure uniform measurements globally.
Crucial conversions: 1 km = 1000 m, 1 m = 100 cm, 1 cm = 10 mm. Non-SI conversions like 1 inch = 2.54 cm are also used.
SI symbols (km, m, cm, mm) are always written in lowercase, followed by a space after the numeric value (e.g., 5 kg).
For accurate measurement, the scale must be in parallel contact with the object, and your eye must be positioned directly above the marking (position B) to avoid parallax error.
Measurements with broken scales involve starting from a full mark and subtracting it from the final reading (Final mark - Initial mark).
Irregular boundaries are measured using thread-alignment techniques or counting wheel rotations (Activity 5.8 Jones Counter principle).
Position is described relative to a fixed reference point; motion is the change in that position over time.
Linear (Rectilinear) Motion: Movement along a straight line path.
Circular Motion: Movement along a circular path about a center.
Oscillatory Motion: To-and-fro movement about a fixed position.
Periodic Motion: Any motion that repeats itself at regular intervals; most circular and oscillatory movements are periodic.
